title: iberis amara subsp. arvatica
intro: Iberis amara subsp. arvatica, also known as Alpine Rockcress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of isothiocyanates and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Iberis amara subsp. arvatica include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
